data.title: As a CI operator running /dso:brainstorm non-interactively, UX probes defer cleanly with a ui_probes:deferred tag
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Define the non-interactive path for the UX probe set in Phase 1 Step 2.5. When `BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false`, skip the UX probe set entirely, emit `INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: UX probes require user input`, and tag the epic `ui_probes:deferred` so a subsequent interactive brainstorm run can complete the probes. The non-interactive path must also bypass the Phase 1 Gate loop-back (S3) on UX-probe-sourced gaps so Phase 1 terminates cleanly in CI.

**Why**: Free-text UX probes cannot be answered in non-interactive mode; without an explicit skip + deferred-tag path, Phase 1 would deadlock on probe questions in CI. The deferred tag preserves the signal for later interactive completion and provides the enforcement surface S8 uses at preplanning entry.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Non-interactive branch in Phase 1 Step 2.5 keyed off `BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false`.
  - Emit `INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: UX probes require user input` (same signal shape as existing INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED emissions in brainstorm).
  - Tag the epic `ui_probes:deferred` via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket edit <epic-id> --tags ...` (follow the existing `interaction:deferred` tag pattern exactly).
  - Bypass Phase 1 Gate loop-back (from S3) on UX-probe-sourced gaps so Phase 1 terminates in non-interactive mode even when intent gaps surface.
  - Regression test invoking brainstorm with `BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false` on a UI seed, asserting (a) `ui_probes:deferred` tag is present on the resulting epic, (b) the probe questions are not asked, (c) Phase 1 terminates without looping.
- OUT:
  - The probe set itself (S2).
  - The loop-back state machine definition (S3).
  - Preplanning-side consumer handling of the tag (S8).
  - Documentation of the tag semantics in CLAUDE.md (S7).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, in non-interactive mode (`BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false`) the UX probe set is skipped entirely.
  ← Satisfies: "In non-interactive mode (BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false), the UX probe set is skipped entirely"
- When this story is complete, in non-interactive mode brainstorm emits `INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: UX probes require user input` and tags the epic `ui_probes:deferred`.
  ← Satisfies: "emit INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED with 'UX probes require user input' and tag the epic ui_probes:deferred so a subsequent interactive brainstorm run can complete them"
- When this story is complete, non-interactive mode bypasses both the UX probe set AND the Phase 1 Gate loop-back on UX-probe-sourced gaps; a regression test asserts Phase 1 terminates in non-interactive mode even when intent gaps surface.
- When this story is complete, a regression test invokes brainstorm with `BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false` on a UI seed and asserts (a) `ui_probes:deferred` tag is present on the resulting epic and (b) the probe questions are not asked.
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by: regression test invokes brainstorm with BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false on a UI seed and asserts (a) ui_probes:deferred tag is present on the resulting epic, (b) the probe questions are not asked"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Follow the existing `interaction:deferred` tag pattern for consistency — tag name is distinct (`ui_probes:deferred`) so consumers can disambiguate; emission mechanism (ticket edit --tags append) is identical.
- [Dependency] Requires S3's loop-back state machine to exist before the non-interactive bypass can be attached to it; also requires S2's probe set and detection code to exist before it can be skipped.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
